one_line: Telugu poet of wit at Vijayanagara, remembered as the sharp-tongued Vikatakavi in Krishnadevaraya’s literary world.

appearance.age_look: mid-30s, lean and wiry build, quick and agile
appearance.face: Oval face with warm medium-brown South Indian complexion, sharp nose, slightly prominent cheekbones, thin arched eyebrows, large alert anime-style brown eyes that glint with mischief, a neat short moustache, default expression a half-smile that looks both amused and calculating.
appearance.hair: Thick black hair oiled and pulled back into a small knotted bun at the back of the head, front hairline clean with a slight widow’s peak, a narrow white vertical Vaishnava-style tilak on the forehead with a small red dot at its base, no turban.
appearance.costume: He wears a simple but neat Vijayanagara-period brahmin scholar’s attire: an off-white cotton angavastram draped over both shoulders and across his chest, edged with a thin maroon border; a calf-length pale cream dhoti wrapped in the South Indian panchakacham style with a deep mustard-yellow border; a fitted short-sleeved light-cream upper garment beneath the drape to keep him modest; around his torso a sacred thread is suggested subtly under the cloth rather than bare; on his wrists a few plain wooden tulasi bead bracelets, and around his neck a single strand of small rudraksha-style beads; his feet are in simple dark-brown leather paduka-style sandals; fabrics look handwoven with a slight texture, with very minimal patterning beyond the borders to keep him visually grounded yet humble.
appearance.signature: A palm-leaf manuscript bundle tied with a red cotton cord and a slender reed stylus tucked into the cord, which he often holds casually in one hand as both his poet’s tool and symbolic ‘weapon’ of wit.

ref_desc: Tenali Ramakrishna is a lean mid-30s Telugu poet with medium-brown skin, oiled black hair in a small bun, Vaishnava tilak, off-white angavastram, cream dhoti with mustard border, simple beads, brown sandals, and a palm-leaf manuscript bundle with reed stylus in maroon and mustard tones.
sheet_prompt: Draw a full-body anime-style model sheet of Tenali Ramakrishna as a dignified yet mischievous Vijayanagara-era Telugu poet: a lean man in his mid-30s with medium-brown South Indian complexion, oval face, sharp nose, thin arched brows, large alert brown eyes, a neat short moustache, and a default half-smile that looks amused and calculating. His thick black hair is oiled and pulled back into a small knotted bun at the back, with a narrow white vertical Vaishnava tilak and tiny red dot on his forehead. He wears an off-white cotton angavastram with a thin maroon border draped over both shoulders and across his chest, over a modest light-cream short-sleeved upper garment, and a calf-length cream dhoti in South Indian panchakacham style with a deep mustard-yellow border; the sacred thread is only hinted at under the cloth to keep him fully covered. Add a single strand of small brown beads around his neck, a couple of simple wooden bracelets, and dark-brown paduka-style sandals. His signature prop is a palm-leaf manuscript bundle tied with a red cotton cord, with a slender reed stylus tucked into the cord and held in one hand as his ‘weapon’ of wit. Use a grounded palette of off-white and cream with mustard-yellow borders, maroon edging, and dark-brown sandals and hair, showing front, side, and back views to clearly display his consistent face, hair, draped garments, and manuscript bundle.
